steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage establishes a cause-effect relationship: sleek, streamlined bodies in modern planes lead to greater aerodynamic efficiency. The key signal is the result described in the second sentence — the sleek design directly explains why modern planes are more aerodynamic.

steps.title: Match the causal transition
steps.content:
Choice C, 'As a result,' directly ties the streamlined design to increased aerodynamics by signaling causation.

The other three either add irrelevant emphasis (A), reverse the expected relationship (B/D), or introduce an illogical contradiction.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
